meta.name: copilot-byok-private
meta.description: GitHub Copilot CLI BYOK -> Private Azure AI (classic Azure OpenAI + Microsoft Foundry) via internal APIM. Dual-backend, sovereign-cloud aware (Commercial + Government). APIM routes default traffic to Foundry and exposes classic AOAI as a legacy path.

subnets.name: snet-pe
subnets.purpose: Private endpoints (AOAI account PE + Foundry account PE)

subnets.name: snet-dns-in
subnets.purpose: Reserved for Private DNS Resolver inbound endpoint

subnets.name: snet-vm
subnets.purpose: Test VM NIC for manual in-VNet APIM validation

subnets.name: AzureBastionSubnet
subnets.purpose: Azure Bastion host (Basic SKU); /26 minimum required

resources.purpose: Central log sink for APIM diagnostic settings and App Insights backing store.
Receives APIM emit-metric per-developer + per-backend telemetry. Workspace-based.
10.60.0.0/16 hosting APIM, PEs, VPN gateway.
Required for APIM subnet. Mandatory inbound rules per APIM docs.
P2S OpenVPN access for pilot developers. SKU VpnGw1.

privatelink.openai.azure.com or .us, VNet-linked. SHARED by both the AOAI PE and the Foundry PE (a privatelink zone name can be linked to a VNet only once); each account contributes its own A record via its PE zone group.

privatelink.cognitiveservices.azure.com or .us, VNet-linked. Required by the Foundry (kind=AIServices) account PE alongside the openai zone. Post-deploy: verify which zone receives the auto-A-record for the Foundry account in Gov.
privatelink.services.ai.azure.com - Commercial-only third Foundry zone. Empty/skipped in Gov (no privatelink.services.ai.azure.us).
Classic/legacy Azure OpenAI account (kind=OpenAI). publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, disableLocalAuth=true. Consumes the shared openai privatelink zone.
gpt-5.1 (2025-11-13) DataZoneStandard 50 capacity. NOTE: usgovvirginia only offers gpt-5.1 on DataZoneStandard (GlobalStandard not available in Gov).
PE binding AOAI 'account' sub-resource to snet-pe; zone group -> shared openai zone.

Cheap auto-routing tier on the AOAI account: gpt-4.1-mini (2025-04-14) DataZoneStandard 50. dependsOn the primary aoaiDeployment to serialize same-account deployment PUTs (avoids the Gov provisioning race). gpt-5.1-mini does NOT exist in usgovvirginia.

Microsoft Foundry account (kind=AIServices), the DEFAULT backend. Exposes the OpenAI-compatible surface. publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, disableLocalAuth=true. MI audience = cognitiveservices.azure.* (same as classic AOAI).
gpt-5.1 (2025-11-13) DataZoneStandard 50 capacity on the Foundry account.
Cheap auto-routing tier on the Foundry account: gpt-4.1-mini (2025-04-14) DataZoneStandard 50. dependsOn the primary foundryDeployment to serialize same-account deployment PUTs.
Separate PE binding Foundry 'account' sub-resource to snet-pe; zone group -> shared openai zone + cognitiveservices zone (+ services.ai zone in Commercial).

Apex zone azure-api.us (Gov) / azure-api.net (Commercial) - NOT privatelink. A record for the APIM gateway -> Internal private IP (10.60.1.4), VNet-linked so in-VNet clients resolve the gateway with no hosts edit. Prerequisite for the VPN DNS-resolver/NRPT phase.
Developer SKU, virtualNetworkType=Internal, system-assigned MI, App Insights logger.

Created once (service-scoped) for all APIs: entra-openid-config-url, api-app-id-uri, api-audience (v2-token aud GUID), required-scope, aoai-default-api-version, aoai-private-base-url, aoai-mi-audience, foundry-private-base-url, foundry-mi-audience, aoai-pinned-models, plus the auto-routing knobs: auto-route-sentinel, auto-route-mini-deployment, auto-route-full-deployment, auto-route-length-threshold, auto-route-ambiguous-band, auto-route-classifier-enabled, auto-route-classifier-deployment (tunable without redeploying policies).

DEFAULT API. name=copilot-byok-foundry, path=openai. Frontend /openai/v1/{chat/completions,completions,embeddings} (model in request body, OpenAI-style). subscriptionRequired + subscriptionKeyParameterNames(header/query=api-key) when authMode=subscriptionKey.

authMode picks the policy file: byok-foundry-policy.xml (jwt) or byok-foundry-policy-subkey.xml (subscriptionKey, default). Both: parse model(from body) -> guard -> emit-metric -> rate-limit -> strip creds -> MODEL-OVERRIDE ROUTING (aoai-pinned-models) -> MI auth for chosen backend -> rewrite-uri to /openai/deployments/{model}/<op> -> set api-version. jwt variant adds validate-jwt + derives developer dims from oid/upn; subscriptionKey variant relies on native APIM subscription validation + derives developer dims from the subscription id/name. Outbound: prompt/completion token metrics with backend dimension.

LEGACY API. name=copilot-byok-aoai, path=aoai (was openai). Frontend /aoai/v1/{chat/completions,completions,embeddings}. Always routes to the classic AOAI account. subscriptionRequired + subscriptionKeyParameterNames(header/query=api-key) when authMode=subscriptionKey.

authMode picks the policy file: byok-aoai-policy.xml (jwt) or byok-aoai-policy-subkey.xml (subscriptionKey, default). Inbound: [jwt: validate-jwt] parse model(from body) -> guard -> emit-metric -> rate-limit -> strip creds -> MI auth (aoai-mi-audience) -> rewrite-uri to /openai/deployments/{model}/<op> -> set api-version -> set-backend-service AOAI private FQDN. Outbound: prompt/completion token metrics.

Rate-limit TIERS as APIM products (byok-standard, byok-power). Each product = subscriptionRequired/published + both APIs linked + a product-scope policy carrying three throttles keyed on the subscription: rate-limit-by-key (burst calls/min), azure-openai-token-limit (TPM, the AI-cost guard), quota-by-key (hard 30-day call ceiling). Developers are GROUPED by which product their subscription is scoped to. Numbers come from the productTiers param. Skipped in jwt mode (jwt policies carry per-oid limits instead).

Ready-to-use test developer subscriptions (dev1->byok-standard, dev2->byok-power) scoped to a PRODUCT so each inherits that product's throttle tier and a key valid for every API in the product. state=active, keys auto-generated by APIM. Each subscription's Name is stamped onto telemetry as the developer (developer_upn). Skipped entirely in jwt mode.
APIM system MI -> 'Cognitive Services OpenAI User' on the AOAI account scope. Gated off (assignAoaiRbac=false) when deployer lacks roleAssignments/write; granted out-of-band.
APIM system MI -> 'Cognitive Services OpenAI User' on the Foundry account scope. Plus optional deployer -> 'Cognitive Services OpenAI Contributor' on each account when deployerPrincipalId is set.

Each principal in playgroundPrincipalIds (User or Group) -> 'Cognitive Services OpenAI User' on BOTH the AOAI and Foundry account scopes. Required for humans to use the portal playground / SDK directly, because local auth (API keys) is disabled. Reachable only from inside the VNet (publicNetworkAccess=Disabled). Empty by default.
NIC for the test VM in snet-vm.
Windows Server 2022 (Standard_D2as_v6 - B2ms/D2s_v3 not available in usgovvirginia) for manual in-VNet APIM testing. Reached via Bastion; tear down when done.
Standard static public IP for Azure Bastion.
Basic SKU Bastion host for browser-based RDP to the test VM without exposing a public IP on the VM.

outOfBand.githubEgressAllowlist: docs/github-egress-allowlist.md (corporate proxy/firewall change, not in Bicep)

decisions: authMode parameter (subscriptionKey default | jwt) selects the caller credential without changing backends. Default subscriptionKey because the customer's sales-led design already standardized on per-developer APIM subscription keys and likely has them provisioned. jwt mode is retained as an opt-in stronger control (per-user Entra identity, ~1h tokens, instant revocation).
Dual-backend fork: deployAoai + deployFoundry switches let a customer deploy classic Azure OpenAI (kind=OpenAI), Microsoft Foundry (kind=AIServices), or both. Both default true for the pilot.

Microsoft Foundry (kind=AIServices) is the DEFAULT backend, exposed at APIM path /openai. Classic AOAI is preserved as a LEGACY backend at path /aoai so existing traffic/maps stay intact while new traffic defaults to Foundry.

Foundry uses the OpenAI-compatible surface, so its MI audience and role are identical to classic AOAI (Cognitive Services OpenAI User on cognitiveservices.azure.*). Native Foundry surface (ai.azure.*) is NOT used.

Model-based routing on the default Foundry API: aoaiPinnedModels (comma list) reroutes specific model names from Foundry to the classic AOAI backend in-policy, on top of the separate /aoai legacy path.

Separate PEs per account (clean isolation), but the openai privatelink zone is SHARED because a privatelink zone name can only be linked to a VNet once; Foundry adds its own cognitiveservices zone (and services.ai in Commercial).
APIM Developer SKU for pilot (no SLA) instead of Premium (~$2.8k/mo). Param-switchable.

In authMode=jwt the wrapper script mints a fresh Entra JWT per invocation (no long-running refresher in v1). In authMode=subscriptionKey (default) the wrapper just carries the long-lived per-developer APIM subscription key in the api-key header.
Both clouds parameterized; first deploy targets usgovvirginia in an Azure Government tenant.
Test VM + Basic Bastion added behind deployTestVm flag for manual APIM validation before standing up the P2S VPN gateway. Ephemeral - intended to be torn down after testing.

Tiered auto model-routing: callers may send the sentinel model 'auto' (autoRouteSentinel) to let APIM pick the cheap mini tier (gpt-4.1-mini) for short non-coding prompts and the full model (gpt-5.1) otherwise. Level 1 is an in-policy heuristic (prompt length vs autoRouteLengthThreshold +/- autoRouteAmbiguousBand, plus a coding-signal regex) with zero added latency; Level 2 is an OPTIONAL classifier-model call (autoRouteClassifierEnabled, off by default) that hits the mini deployment with max_tokens:1 only on the ambiguous band and fails safe to the full model on any error. Routing lives in all four policy variants (subscriptionKey/jwt x Foundry/AOAI); the resolved deployment overwrites the body 'model' and is recorded via an auto_route metric dimension. Requires deployMiniModel (a second mini deployment per backend, serialized via dependsOn). gpt-5.1-mini is unavailable in usgovvirginia so gpt-4.1-mini (2025-04-14, DataZoneStandard) is the Gov cheap tier.
